services_upgradeWarning: Upgrade to {tag} (may require manual data migration, review the service docs first)
services_migrateExplain: Migrate to {tag} via dump+restore (safer than Upgrade for data-incompatible jumps)
services_rollbackExplain: Roll back to {tag} (the image running before the last update)
sites_confirmUnlink: Unlink {domain}? The site will stop being served by nginx.
sites_controls_queueToggle_failing: Queue worker is failing, click to restart
sites_controls_horizonToggle_failing: Horizon is failing, click to restart
services_env_connectHint: Use {loopback4} or {loopback6} from your host. Inside containers use the hostname shown in the .env block below.
services_env_none: This service has no env vars.
services_banner_description: {description}. Installs as a custom service that auto-starts {svcName}.
services_banner_dismiss: Dismiss, you can still add this from the Services + button
services_preset_subtitle: Bundled, opt-in services. Installing one adds it as a custom service so you can start, stop, or remove it from the Services tab.
system_dns_fixHint: Try {start}, or run {cmd} to fix DNS.
system_dns_quietDefault: dnsmasq is quiet by default. Add {option} to {path} and restart lerd-dns to see resolved queries here.
system_watcher_description: Monitors parked directories, site files, git worktrees, and DNS health. Logs WARN and ERROR events by default; set {env} in the service environment for verbose output.
system_watcher_quiet: No events yet, watcher is running quietly.
system_php_stopWarn: {count} site(s) use this version, they will stop working
system_node_managedBySystemHint: Installing a version below will switch to lerd-managed Node.js via fnm and override your system node.
system_node_cannotRemove: Cannot remove, sites are using this version
system_node_cannotRemoveSystem: Node is managed by the system
system_node_installNewHint: Enter a major version (e.g. {major}) or a specific one (e.g. {specific}). Installed via fnm.
system_lerd_updateHint: Lerd updates by re-running its installer, which needs an interactive terminal so it can prompt for sudo and show the download progress. Click below to open one with the update command pre-filled, or run {cmd} in any terminal yourself.
system_workerMode_description: Controls how framework workers (queue, schedule, horizon, reverb, custom) are launched on macOS. The default is exec mode for lower memory.
system_workerMode_exec_description: One podman exec per worker into the shared FPM container, supervised by launchd with a pid-file dedup guard. Near-zero memory overhead per worker. Matches the Linux profile.
system_workerMode_container_description: One detached container per worker, spawned from the FPM image. Higher memory (especially for Horizon which supervises its own pool), but 1:1 supervisor boundary with podman's own restart policy.
system_workerMode_note_body: Changing the mode stops every active worker in its old shape, cleans up the on-disk artifacts, and restarts it in the new shape. Other lerd services (FPM, nginx, DNS, built-in services) aren't touched.
system_workerMode_confirm_body: Switch from {from} to {to}? Every active worker will be stopped, its on-disk artifacts cleaned up, and restarted in the new shape.
system_workerMode_confirm_hint: FPM, nginx, DNS, and built-in services are not touched. This may take a few seconds.
system_workerMode_apply_failed: Could not switch worker runtime. Workers were not changed.
system_autostart_description: When enabled, lerd-ui and lerd-watcher start automatically on every login.
system_lan_exposedDescription: Sites are reachable from other devices on the LAN at {ip}. The dnsmasq resolver answers {pattern} with this address. Database, cache, and mail containers stay on loopback only ({loop4} and {loop6} on dual-stack hosts), Laravel apps reach them through the internal bridge, no need to expose them to the network.
system_lan_loopbackDescription: Lerd is bound to loopback only ({loop4} and {loop6} on dual-stack hosts), invisible to other devices on the network. Safe for untrusted wifi (cafés, conference networks). Expose to share your sites with another machine on a trusted LAN.
system_lan_macosWarning: macOS: This toggle only controls DNS resolution ({pattern} → 127.0.0.1 or LAN IP). Ports 80 and 443 are always reachable from the LAN due to a gvproxy limitation, the Podman Machine network layer does not support IP-specific bindings for privileged ports. Service ports (MySQL, Redis, etc.) are properly restricted to 127.0.0.1.
system_lan_progress_doneExposed: Lerd is now exposed on the LAN at {ip}.
system_lan_progress_doneHidden: Lerd is back on loopback only.
system_lan_postExpose_firewall: Make sure your firewall allows port 5300 from the devices you want to grant access.
system_lan_remote_hint: Generate a single-use code (15 min TTL) the machine can use to install the lerd root CA and configure its DNS resolver in one shot.
system_lan_remote_bullet1: Installs {mkcert} if missing and trusts the lerd root CA so HTTPS sites validate.
system_lan_remote_bullet2: Detects the active resolver (NetworkManager+dnsmasq, systemd-resolved, standalone dnsmasq, or macOS {resolver}) and points {test} at this server's DNS on port 5300.
system_lan_remote_bullet3: Runs entirely over the LAN, no traffic leaves your network.
system_lan_remote_footer: The endpoint is restricted to RFC 1918 source IPs and the code is single-use. Click {generate} again for a fresh code.
system_remote_description: When disabled, only loopback ({loop4} and {loop6}) can reach the dashboard. Enable to grant LAN clients access with HTTP Basic auth. Loopback always bypasses authentication, so you can never lock yourself out from this machine.
system_remote_inertWarning: Credentials are stored but inert, LAN exposure is off, so LAN clients still get 403. Run {cmd} (or click {btn} above) to make them active.
system_remote_enableDisabledHint: Run lan:expose first, without it the dashboard is loopback-only and credentials would be inert.
system_remote_exposeFirst: Expose lerd to the LAN first. Dashboard credentials are only meaningful while the dashboard is reachable from other devices.
system_remoteModal_title: Enable LAN access to the dashboard
system_remoteModal_subtitle: Set a username and password. LAN clients will need to present them via HTTP Basic auth to reach the dashboard. Loopback access from this machine always bypasses the gate, so you can never lock yourself out.
system_language_description: The interface language. Defaults to your browser language; falls back to English.
apps_empty: No service dashboards are active. Start a service with a dashboard (e.g. mailpit, rustfs, phpMyAdmin) to see it here.
